mergeRationale: The patch calls np.asarray() on DataArray payloads, which eagerly materializes dask-backed data and drops chunk semantics during update.

requiredFindings.summary: The patch calls np.asarray() on a DataArray, forcing dask-backed data to compute eagerly.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: Dataset.update should preserve chunking and lazy evaluation; materializing can be slow, memory-heavy, and semantically wrong.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Use DataArray.data when constructing the Variable, or warn/error without computing values.

requiredFindings.summary: The test compares .values, which itself computes dask arrays.
requiredFindings.expectedReasoning: It cannot catch the chunk-preservation regression.
requiredFindings.acceptableFix: Assert the updated variable still has chunks and retains a dask-backed array.

disallowedConclusions: The patch can be merged because the updated values are equal.
np.asarray is safe here because DataArray can always be converted to NumPy.
